text: The Mishnaic phrase lo alecha hamlacha ligmor — "it is not upon you to complete the task" — functions as a structural principle, not merely consolation. In rabbinic thought, the universe itself is considered incomplete; creation was left unfinished so that humanity could become partners in its ongoing repair, a concept the Talmud calls tikkun olam. This framing transforms the individual's partial contribution from a failure into a moral necessity. You are not expected to finish because finishing was never the architecture of meaningful work. The task exceeds any single life; what is required is unbroken participation.
question: According to the passage, why does the Talmud frame the universe as incomplete?

options.text: To explain why Torah study can never be fully mastered
So that humanity can become partners in its ongoing repair
To discourage scholars from pursuing personal recognition
Because creation was disrupted by human disobedience
